options.label: 3.2 cm Slant Pocket
options.description: A slant pocket has its opening cut on an angle, running diagonally from the waistband toward the side seam, with a standard ~3.2cm opening. The angle gives easy hand access and helps the pocket lie flat against the hip without gaping when sitting, making it the most common front-pocket style on tailored trousers. It reads as slightly more contemporary and sporty than a straight on-seam pocket.

options.label: On-Seam (Side Seam)
options.description: An on-seam (vertical) pocket hides its opening directly within the trouser's side seam, so the entry is virtually invisible from the front. This creates the cleanest, most formal silhouette with no line interrupting the front of the trouser, though access is slightly less convenient than a slant. It is the traditional choice for dress and suit trousers.

options.label: 2.5 cm Slant Pocket
options.description: 2.5 cm angled entry pocket.

options.label: No Front Pockets
options.description: Clean front — formal eveningwear.

options.label: Besom / Jetted Pocket
options.description: A jetted or besom front pocket is finished with two narrow fabric welts (double-jetted) framing a clean horizontal or angled slit, with no flap. It is the most refined and minimal pocket treatment, mirroring the jetted pockets seen on the rear of dress trousers and on jacket breast pockets. The result is a sleek, formal opening that sits flush with the cloth.

options.label: 2.0 cm Slant Pocket
options.description: Narrow 2.0 cm angled pocket opening.

options.label: 5.1 cm Slant Pocket
options.description: Wide 5.1 cm slant — generous opening.

options.label: Welt Front Pocket
options.description: A welt front pocket uses a single sewn-in welt strip to reinforce and frame the opening, giving a defined but still tailored edge. It is a half-step between the invisible on-seam pocket and the decorative jetted pocket, offering a neat, structured opening. Often used as a slanted or offset front pocket on trousers and odd jackets.

options.label: 2.0 cm Slant with Stripe
options.description: Slant pocket with contrast stripe trim.

options.label: 2.5 cm Slant with Stripe
options.description: 2.5 cm slant with contrast stripe.

options.label: 3.2 cm Slant with Stripe
options.description: 3.2 cm slant with contrast stripe detail.

options.label: 5.1 cm Slant with Stripe
options.description: Wide slant with contrast stripe.

options.label: Square Jeans Pocket
options.description: The square jeans pocket is the classic Western five-pocket front opening: a near-vertical, squared-off curve reinforced with rivets and topstitching. It is the most casual and rugged front-pocket style, signalling denim and workwear heritage rather than tailoring. The squared shape sits tight to the body and is built for durability.

options.label: Diamond Jeans Pocket
options.description: Diamond-shaped jeans pocket.

options.label: Arc Jeans Pocket
options.description: The arc (curved) jeans pocket is the slash-style five-pocket opening, sweeping in a smooth curve from the waistband down to the side seam. The rounded mouth gives easier hand access than the squared version and softens the front line slightly while keeping the casual denim character. It is the most common front pocket on modern jeans and casual cotton trousers.

options.label: Round Jeans Pocket
options.description: Rounded jeans pocket — casual style.

answers.label: Slanted
answers.description: Angled opening — easy access, sporty line.

answers.label: Side-Seam
answers.description: Hidden in the seam — the cleanest look.

answers.label: Jetted / Welt
answers.description: Sharp horizontal welt — formal and tidy.

options.label: Shallow (20 cm)
options.description: Shallow — minimizes hip bulk.

options.label: Standard (23 cm)
options.description: Standard depth — versatile.

options.label: Deep (26 cm)
options.description: Deep pocket — practical for larger items.

options.label: No Topstitch (Hidden Stitch)
options.description: Clean hidden pocket edge.

options.label: 0.6 cm Topstitch
options.description: Single topstitch line.

options.label: 0.1 + 0.7 cm Double Topstitch
options.description: Two parallel stitch lines.

options.label: 0.15 cm Topstitch
options.description: Fine 0.15 cm topstitch.

options.label: 0.6 cm Pick Stitch
options.description: Hand-imitation pick stitch.

options.label: 0.15 cm Pick Stitch
options.description: Fine pick stitch detail.

options.label: None
options.description: No watch pocket.

options.label: Regular Right
options.description: Small right-side watch fob pocket.

options.label: Left Welt
options.description: Left welt watch pocket.

options.label: Both Sides
options.description: Watch pocket on each side.

options.label: Right Welt
options.description: Right welt-style watch pocket.

options.label: Right Besom
options.description: Right besom watch pocket.

options.label: Left Besom
options.description: Left besom watch pocket.

options.label: Right with Diamond Flap
options.description: Right watch pocket with diamond flap and button.

options.label: None
options.description: No coin pocket.

options.label: Right Side
options.description: Coin pocket on the right.

options.label: Left Side
options.description: Coin pocket on the left.

options.label: Both Sides
options.description: Coin pockets on both sides.

options.label: I-Shape Bartack
options.description: Standard I-shaped reinforcement tack at pocket.

options.label: D-Shape Bartack
options.description: D-shaped reinforcement tack.

options.label: Dash (–) Bartack
options.description: Minimal dash-style reinforcement.

options.label: Hip Pocket I-Edge
options.description: I-shaped edge reinforcement on hip pocket.

options.label: Standard Waistband
options.description: A standard waistband is a single band of self-fabric, typically 4cm wide, closing on a central button and continuous to a belt-loop or side-fastening trouser. It is the default, versatile choice that suits virtually any trouser style and sits cleanly under a jacket. Nothing about it draws attention, which is exactly its appeal.

options.label: Extended Tab Waistband
options.description: An extended tab waistband carries the band past the centre front into a tab that fastens off-centre, usually onto a hidden button or hook a few inches across. It gives a clean, finished front for beltless trousers and adds a touch of sartorial flair while reinforcing the closure. The tab is a hallmark of higher-end tailored and military-inspired trousers.

options.label: Waistcoat Back Band
options.description: A waistcoat-back (or fishtail) band rises higher at the centre back into a split, V-shaped extension designed to be worn with braces, often fitted with a cinch buckle. The higher back keeps the shirt tucked and the trouser anchored, and the fishtail allows brace buttons to sit cleanly. It is a traditional, formal detail associated with high-rise British tailoring.

options.label: Elasticated Insert
options.description: An elasticated insert hides a section of elastic, usually at the sides or back of the waistband, allowing the waist to flex a little with the body. It adds comfort and a forgiving fit without the visible bulk of a fully elastic waist, and is increasingly common on softer, casual tailored trousers. The give makes it ideal for travel and all-day wear.

options.label: Double Waistband
options.description: A double (or wider, layered) waistband uses a deeper band, sometimes split into two visible rows of stitching or two stacked bands, that frames the waist more prominently. It gives extra support and a sharper, more architectural line at the top of the trouser, flattering a high rise. The added height is a signature of vintage-inspired and Neapolitan high-waist trousers.

options.label: Trouser Waist (No Band)
options.description: A no-waistband trouser finishes the top edge with the cloth simply turned and faced rather than carrying a separate band. This creates an exceptionally clean, uninterrupted line from waist to hip, often seen on the most minimal modern designs and on certain casual drawstring trousers. Without a band the waist relies on the facing and closure for shape.

options.label: 3.5 cm
options.description: Narrow band — modern silhouette.

options.label: 3.8 cm
options.description: Slim-standard band width.

options.label: 4.0 cm
options.description: Standard width — balanced.

options.label: 4.5 cm
options.description: Wide band — classic British trouser.

options.label: 5.0 cm
options.description: Very wide — vintage proportions.

options.label: Sharp, Hook & Button
options.description: A hook-and-button closure fastens the waistband tab with a concealed metal hook-and-bar backed up by a button, giving a flat, secure front with no visible bulge. The hook takes the strain so the waistband sits smooth and the button adds a backup fixing. It is a clean, quality closure typical of tailored trousers.

options.label: Round, Hook & Button
options.description: Rounded tab with hook and button.

options.label: Square, Hook & Button
options.description: Square tab with hook and button.

options.label: No Extension, Button Only
options.description: No extension tab, button closure only.

options.label: No Extension, Hook Only
options.description: No extension tab, hook closure only.

options.label: Double Sharp Extension
options.description: Double-width pointed extension tab.

options.label: Double Round Extension
options.description: Double rounded extension.

options.label: Double Square Extension
options.description: Double square extension tab.

options.label: Sharp, Double Hooks
options.description: Pointed tab with two hooks.

options.label: Round, Double Hooks
options.description: Rounded tab with two hooks.

options.label: Square, Double Hooks
options.description: Square tab with two hooks.

options.label: Sharp, Double Button & Hook
options.description: Pointed extension with double button and hook.

options.label: Round, Double Button & Hook
options.description: Rounded extension with double button and hook.

options.label: Square, Double Button & Hook
options.description: Square extension with double button and hook.

options.label: Double Extension, Hook & Button
options.description: A double extension waistband carries two overlapping extended tabs that fasten across the front, doubling the closure and creating an especially clean, supported waist. The layered tabs distribute strain and give a sharp, finished front for beltless wear. It is a refined detail on high-waisted bespoke and military-style trousers.

options.label: HW Pointed Extension with Buckle
options.description: Hardware pointed extension with adjustable buckle.

options.label: 5 cm
options.description: A short 5 cm waistband extension — the closure tab reaches 5 cm past the fly. The shortest, cleanest extension, sitting almost flush behind the fly.

options.label: 5.5 cm
options.description: A 5.5 cm waistband extension tab reaching just past the fly closure for a clean, secure front.

options.label: 6 cm
options.description: A 6 cm waistband extension tab — a moderate reach past the closure, the common tailored extension length.

options.label: 6.5 cm
options.description: A 6.5 cm waistband extension tab, reaching a little further across the front for added support.

options.label: 7 cm
options.description: A 7 cm waistband extension tab carrying the closure well past the fly for a flat, supported waist.

options.label: 13 cm
options.description: A long 13 cm waistband extension — a deep French-bearer-style tab reaching far across the front for a very secure, anchored waist.

options.label: 15 cm
options.description: The longest 15 cm waistband extension — a full French-bearer tab spanning well past the fly, the most supported and structured closure.

options.label: Standard (No Adjuster)
options.description: No side adjusters — belt loops only.

options.label: Adjust Buckle
options.description: Side adjustment buckle.

options.label: Square Side Adjust Button
options.description: Square button adjusters on both sides.

options.label: Adjustable Buckle with Belt Loop
options.description: Buckle adjuster alongside belt loop.

options.label: Standard (7 Loops)
options.description: Standard seven belt loops space evenly around the waistband to carry a belt securely, the default on most casual and odd trousers. The loops let a belt do the holding while keeping the trouser anchored at common stress points. Seven is the typical full complement for a balanced layout.

options.label: 5 Loops
options.description: Fewer loops — cleaner look.

options.label: No Belt Loops
options.description: No belt loops leaves a clean, uninterrupted waistband, relying on side adjusters or braces for support. The absence of loops gives the dressiest, most refined waist and prevents a belt from breaking the line between trouser and jacket. It is standard on formal and bespoke tailored trousers.

options.label: Double / Passant Loops
options.description: Double (passant) belt loops use pairs of narrow loops at each position instead of single ones, a military and equestrian-inspired detail. The doubled loops hold a belt more firmly and add a distinctive, slightly utilitarian look to the waistband. They are a deliberate styling flourish on field and casual trousers.

options.label: 2.0 cm Width Loops
options.description: Wider 2.0 cm belt loops.

options.label: One Loop Right Front Only
options.description: Single right-front loop — minimalist dress trouser.

options.label: X-Style Front Loops
options.description: Crossed X-style loops on front, standard elsewhere.

options.label: No Back Detail
options.description: Plain back waistband — no adjustment tab.

options.label: Square Adjustment Tab with Buckle
options.description: HW square adjustment tab at center back waist.

options.label: Pointed Adjustment Tab with Buttons
options.description: HW pointed adjustment tab with four buttons at back waist.

options.label: Flat Front (No Dart)
options.description: A flat front has no pleats or darts at all, so the cloth runs smooth and uninterrupted from the waistband down the front. It gives the cleanest, most modern and slimming silhouette and pairs naturally with a plain hem. It works best on slimmer cuts where there is no excess fabric to manage.

options.label: Flat Front, Single Dart
options.description: A flat front with a single dart adds one short, stitched-down dart on each side of the front to shape the waist into the hip without an open pleat. It keeps the smooth flat-front look while improving the fit over a curved seat and thigh. The dart removes excess cloth cleanly rather than letting it fold.

options.label: Flat Front, Double Dart
options.description: A flat front with double darts uses two parallel darts per side to draw in more waist suppression while keeping a pleat-free front. It allows a fuller seat and thigh to be fitted to a trimmer waist with a still-clean front face. The result is shaping closer to a pleat's roominess but with a flat, tailored appearance.

options.label: Single Forward Pleat
options.description: A single forward pleat is one fold per side that opens inward toward the fly, the direction associated with English tailoring. It adds room across the front of the thigh that collapses when standing, keeping a streamlined, slimming front line. Drawing the eye inward, forward pleats look narrower and tidier than reverse ones.

options.label: Double Forward Pleats
options.description: Double forward pleats add a second, smaller inward-facing fold between the main pleat and the pocket, helping the main pleat stay closed. They provide generous room through the hip and thigh for a fuller, more classic drape while preserving the neat inward-facing English line. This is a traditional dressy detail seen on high-rise tailored trousers.

options.label: Single Reverse Pleat
options.description: A single reverse pleat is one fold per side that opens outward toward the pockets, the style associated with Italian tailoring and most ready-to-wear. It adds comfortable thigh room and keeps the inward half of the leg neat, with many considering it the more attractive of the two. It is the most common pleat found on off-the-peg trousers.

options.label: Double Reverse Pleats
options.description: Double reverse pleats use two outward-facing folds per side, giving the fullest, roomiest front and a relaxed, voluminous drape through the hip and thigh. The outward direction emphasises width, making this a bold, classic choice on wide and high-rise trousers. It is the most generous pleat configuration in common use.

answers.label: Flat Front
answers.description: Clean and contemporary — a slim silhouette.

answers.label: Single Pleat
answers.description: One forward pleat — comfort with elegance.

options.label: 1.5 cm
options.description: Shallow pleat — subtle volume.

options.label: 2.0 cm
options.description: Standard narrow pleat.

options.label: 2.5 cm
options.description: Medium pleat depth — balanced.

options.label: 3.2 cm
options.description: Deep pleat — generous volume.

options.label: 3.8 cm
options.description: Very deep pleat — dramatic drape.

options.label: Zip Fly (Nylon)
options.description: A nylon (coil) zip fly uses a lightweight plastic-toothed zipper, which is thin, flexible and lies very flat behind the fly. It keeps the front of the trouser smooth with no visible hardware and is quiet and snag-resistant. The low profile makes it well suited to lighter-weight and summer cloths where bulk would show.

options.label: Zip Fly (Metal)
options.description: A metal zip fly uses metal teeth, prized for durability and a more substantial, secure feel under heavier cloths. It is the workhorse closure for sturdier trousers and denim, standing up to repeated use better than a coil zip. The trade-off is slightly more bulk and weight behind the fly.

options.label: Button Fly
options.description: A button fly closes with a row of buttons concealed behind the fly placket, the traditional pre-zip method still chosen for its clean drape and heritage character. It has no metal to fail and lets the front of the trouser fall in a smoother line, at the cost of being slower to fasten. It is common on bespoke trousers and selvedge denim alike.

options.label: Fish-Mouth Fly
options.description: A fish-mouth (frogmouth) fly refers to the curved, fish-mouth shaped opening at the top of the fly where the waistband closes, often paired with frogmouth front pockets. The shaped notch lets the waistband fasten cleanly and lie flat over the stomach. It is a vintage tailoring detail seen on mid-century trousers.

options.label: Fish-Claw Fly
options.description: Fish-claw shaped fly front.

options.label: French Fly
options.description: A French fly adds an extra inner tab that extends across from the waistband and fastens with a hidden button inside the fly. This anchors the waistband closed and flat, taking strain off the main closure and preventing the front from pulling open. It is a mark of quality construction on better tailored trousers.

options.label: Straight Fly
options.description: Straight topstitched fly — military style.

options.label: Pointed Fly
options.description: A pointed fly finishes the top of the fly placket with a tailored point rather than a square or curved end, adding a subtle decorative shape where the waistband meets the fly. It is a refined detail that signals hand-finishing on bespoke and made-to-measure trousers. The point sits neatly under the waistband closure.

options.label: Curved Fly Stitch
options.description: Standard curved topstitch on fly.

options.label: Straight Fly Stitch
options.description: Clean straight topstitch.

options.label: No Fly Topstitch
options.description: No decorative topstitch on fly.

options.label: Standard Bar Tacks
options.description: Reinforcement at stress points.

options.label: No Bar Tacks
options.description: Clean finish without visible tacks.

options.label: Pressed Crease (Standard)
options.description: Sharp pressed front crease — formal.

options.label: No Crease
options.description: Soft creaseless front — casual trousers.

options.label: Fused Crease (Permanent)
options.description: Permanently fused crease — stays sharp.

options.label: No Back Pockets
options.description: No back pockets leaves the seat of the trouser completely clean and uninterrupted, the most minimal and formal rear treatment. Without pocket openings the cloth lies smoothly over the seat, which flatters dress trousers and avoids sagging from carried items. It is favoured on the dressiest tailored and evening trousers.

options.label: Left Welt with Button
options.description: A welt back pocket with a button has a single reinforcing welt across the opening and a button closure to secure the contents. The welt gives a clean, slightly raised edge while the button keeps the pocket shut and adds a discreet detail. It is a tidy, traditional choice for dress trousers.

options.label: Both Welt Pockets (Left Button)
options.description: Both sides welt with button.

options.label: Left Besom with Button & Buttonhole
options.description: Single left besom pocket.

options.label: Double Besom
options.description: Double besom pockets both sides.

options.label: Left Pointed Patch Pocket
options.description: A pointed patch back pocket is sewn onto the outside of the seat with a shaped, pointed bottom edge, the casual style seen on jeans and chinos. Being applied on top of the cloth it is the most informal and visible back pocket, often topstitched for emphasis. It signals workwear and casual heritage rather than tailoring.

options.label: Both Pointed Patch Pockets
options.description: Two pointed patch back pockets.

options.label: Besom with Button
options.description: A besom (double-jetted) back pocket with a button frames the opening with two narrow welts and fastens with a button, the standard refined rear pocket on dress trousers. The clean piped opening sits flush with the seat and the button adds security without bulk. It is the most common and most formal back-pocket style.

options.label: Welt with Zipper
options.description: Welt pocket with zipper closure.

options.label: Plain Welt (No Button)
options.description: Simple welt pocket, no button.

options.label: Right Besom with Button Only
options.description: Right side besom with button, no left.

options.label: Both Besom (Right Button)
options.description: Both sides besom, button on right side.

options.label: Besom Decorative (No Bag)
options.description: Decorative besom — no functional pocket bag.

options.label: Right Besom Only
options.description: Right side besom only, no left pocket.

options.label: Left Besom Only (No Right)
options.description: Left side besom only, no right pocket.

options.label: Besom with Flap
options.description: A besom back pocket with a flap adds a buttoned flap over the double-jetted opening, combining a clean piped edge with a covered, more secure closure. The flap makes the pocket slightly more casual and protective than a plain besom while staying neat. It suits sportier tailored and country trousers.

options.label: Regular Flap Pocket
options.description: A flap back pocket covers the opening with a buttoned flap, making it more casual and practical than an open jetted pocket. The flap protects the contents and adds a sportier, country look to the seat of the trouser. It is common on tweed, moleskin and other rugged tailored trousers.

options.label: Pointed Flap with Button
options.description: Pointed flap with buttonhole and button.

options.label: Diamond Flap with Button
options.description: Diamond-shaped flap pocket.

options.label: Besom with Round Tab & Button
options.description: Besom pocket with round tab detail.

options.label: Both Besom with Tab & Button
options.description: Both sides besom with tab and button.

options.label: Besom with Tab & Button
options.description: Single besom with tab and button.

options.label: No Darts
options.description: Flat back panel — minimal construction.

options.label: Single Back Darts
options.description: One dart each side — shaping at seat.

options.label: Double Back Darts
options.description: Two darts each side — max seat shaping.

options.label: Open Seam
options.description: Standard open center back seam.

options.label: Flat-Fell Seam
options.description: Double-sewn flat-fell seam — workwear craft.

options.label: Plain Hem 5.0 cm
options.description: A plain hem finishes the trouser leg with a simple turned-and-stitched bottom, with no cuff. It gives the cleanest, most formal line and is the standard for dress, evening and flat-front trousers. The uncluttered hem keeps the eye flowing down to the shoe.

options.label: Plain Hem 6.4 cm
options.description: Slightly wider plain hem allowance.

options.label: 4.4 cm Cuff Turn-Up
options.description: A 4.4cm turn-up cuff folds the hem back on itself to a moderate depth, adding weight that helps the trouser hang and break cleanly. Cuffs are slightly less formal than a plain hem and pair classically with pleated trousers, giving visual balance at the bottom of the leg. The 4.4cm depth is a restrained, traditional width.

options.label: 5.1 cm Cuff Turn-Up
options.description: A 5.1cm (2-inch) turn-up cuff is the deeper, modern-standard cuff popularised by Italian tailoring, adding noticeable weight and presence at the hem. The extra depth makes a confident statement and anchors fuller, pleated and wide-leg trousers especially well. Like all cuffs it is best avoided on the most formal evening trousers.

options.label: Plain Hem 7.0 cm
options.description: 7.0 cm plain hem — generous allowance.

options.label: Plain Hem 7.6 cm
options.description: 7.6 cm plain hem allowance.

options.label: Plain Hem 9.0 cm
options.description: Wide 9.0 cm hem — traditional trouser.

options.label: 3.2 cm Cuff Turn-Up
options.description: Narrow 3.2 cm cuff.

options.label: 3.8 cm Cuff Turn-Up
options.description: Standard 3.8 cm cuff.

options.label: 11.5 cm Extra Fabric (Unfinished)
options.description: Unfinished with 11.5 cm extra for later alterations.

options.label: 13.0 cm Extra Fabric (Unfinished)
options.description: Generous 13.0 cm extra fabric for alterations.

options.label: 4.0 cm Inner Edge Fold (Slanted)
options.description: 4.0 cm slanted inner edge fold hem.

options.label: 7.0 cm Inner Edge Fold (Slanted)
options.description: 7.0 cm slanted inner edge fold — wide trouser.

options.label: Fixed Bottom, 2.5 cm Topstitch
options.description: Fixed hem with visible 2.5 cm topstitch.

options.label: Fringe Edge
options.description: A fringe (frayed/raw) edge leaves the hem unfinished so the weft threads fray into a soft fringe, a deliberately casual and contemporary detail. It softens the bottom line and is seen on relaxed cotton, denim and fashion-forward trousers rather than tailored ones. The raw edge reads as undone and modern.

options.label: Unfinished (Raw Edge)
options.description: Raw, unfinished hem — bespoke avant-garde.

quiz.question: How should the trouser hem finish?

answers.label: Cuffed / Turn-Up
answers.description: Folded turn-up — traditional weight and structure.

options.label: Tapered
options.description: A tapered leg is cut fuller through the hip and thigh and then narrows progressively below the knee to a slimmer hem. It gives a clean, modern line with comfort up top and a trim finish at the ankle, flattering most builds. The taper draws the silhouette neatly toward the shoe.

options.label: Straight Cut
options.description: A straight cut keeps a consistent width from the knee down to the hem, neither narrowing nor flaring. It is the most versatile, timeless leg line, giving a balanced drape that suits nearly every cloth and occasion. The even width creates a clean column from thigh to shoe.

options.label: Wide Leg
options.description: A wide leg keeps a generous, full width all the way down, often with a high rise and pleats, for a relaxed vintage-inspired drape. It moves with the body and creates an elegant, billowing line that flatters with cuffs to add weight. It is the most dramatic and fashion-forward leg shape.

options.label: Slim
options.description: A slim fit is cut close through the hip, thigh and leg for a lean, body-following line all the way to the hem. It gives the sharpest modern silhouette and pairs best with flat fronts and plain hems. The trim cut emphasises a slender, contemporary look.

options.label: No Heel Guard
options.description: No heel reinforcement.

options.label: Heel Guard
options.description: Standard heel guard — protects hem from wear.

options.label: Whole Circle Heel Guard
options.description: Full circle heel guard — maximum protection.

options.label: Whole Circle (Finished Ribbon)
options.description: Full circle heel guard with finished ribbon edge.

options.label: No Lining
options.description: Unlined — lightest and most breathable.

options.label: Front Half (Pocket Inside)
options.description: Front half lining, pocket on inside.

options.label: Front & Back Full (Pocket Inside)
options.description: Complete lining — supreme comfort.

options.label: Front & Back Half (Pocket Inside)
options.description: Half lining on both panels, pocket inside.

options.label: Front Half (Pocket Outside)
options.description: Front half lining, pocket outside.

options.label: Front & Back Half (Pocket Outside)
options.description: Half lining on both panels, pocket outside.

options.label: Front & Back Full (Pocket Outside)
options.description: Complete full lining, pocket outside.

options.label: No Crotch Lining
options.description: No additional lining at crotch.

options.label: Fabric
options.description: Outer fabric applied to back crotch — durable.

options.label: Matched Lining
options.description: Crotch lining matched to body lining.

options.label: Standard Lining
options.description: Standard lining at back crotch area.

options.label: Standard Pocket Lining
options.description: Standard white/neutral pocket bag lining.

options.label: Appointed Pocket Lining
options.description: Specify a custom fabric for pocket bags.

options.label: Standard Curtain Lining
options.description: Fabric curtain inside waistband — standard.

options.label: Pleated Curtain
options.description: Pleated curtain lining — premium drape.

options.label: Appointed Waistband Fabric
options.description: Custom appointed waistband inner fabric.

options.label: Standard with Snugtex
options.description: Curtain lining with non-slip snugtex gripper.

options.label: Standard, Snugtex & Button
options.description: Snugtex with inner shirt-stay button.

options.label: Gripper Strip
options.description: Silicone gripper strip inside waistband.

options.label: Standard, Piping & Snugtex
options.description: Piped edge with snugtex — luxury finish.

options.label: Appointed Lining
options.description: Custom lining fabric inside waistband.

options.label: Pleat with Snugtex & Button
options.description: Pleated lining with snugtex and shirt-stay button.

options.label: Pleat with Griplets
options.description: Pleated lining with griplet fasteners.

options.label: Appointed Finished Waistband
options.description: Pre-finished waistband interior — bespoke.

options.label: Inside Waistband
options.description: Brand label inside the waistband — standard.

options.label: Care Label Only
options.description: Care label only — no brand label visible.

options.label: No Contrast
options.description: Single fabric throughout.

options.label: Side Seam Satin Stripe
options.description: Tuxedo-style satin side stripe.

options.label: Contrast Pocket Bags
options.description: Contrast color pocket bag fabric.

options.label: Contrast Waistband
options.description: Contrasting fabric waistband.
